title: Trace runtime code/version/process identity for low-confidence live opens
subsystem: runtime identity / process truth / confidence gate

context.problem_statement: The current on-disk bridge path appears correctly guarded, yet earlier live runtime still opened trades below the intended 0.60 floor. The most likely explanation is runtime divergence, stale bytecode, wrong process/path, or another code-version identity mismatch.
context.manager_intent: Do not patch logic until the exact runtime code/process identity that produced the low-confidence opens is proven.

objective: Prove exactly what code path / file revision / process identity was running when the low-confidence trades opened, and determine whether runtime divergence explains the live confidence-gate rebreak.
conditionally_allowed_files: Process/runtime artifacts only if needed to establish which executable/script/path was active when trades 241/243/244 opened
launch_all.py only if strictly needed to explain which process/path was spawned
forbidden_actions: Broad repo sweep unrelated to runtime identity
required_work: Establish the exact startup/restart evidence surrounding trades 241/243/244
Compare current on-disk source against any runtime/version evidence available from logs, pycache metadata, or process-launch traces
Determine whether stale bytecode, wrong process, wrong working tree, or another runtime divergence best explains the low-confidence opens
If pycache metadata exists, inspect timestamps/relationships read-only
If another file must be read to prove the spawned path/process identity, keep that scope expansion minimal and explicitly justify it
questions: 1. What exact process/runtime/startup evidence surrounds trades 241/243/244?
2. Is there evidence that the running code at the time differed from the current on-disk code?
3. Is stale pycache still a plausible cause, or can it now be ruled in/out more strongly?
4. Is the launcher/process path spawning a different file or working tree than expected?
5. What exact next fix target should be opened after this trace?
acceptance_criteria: Runtime/startup evidence established as far as the allowed artifacts support
Explicit judgment on runtime divergence likelihood
Explicit ruling on whether stale pycache / wrong process / wrong path remains plausible
